Cleanup\n\nuse crate::anti_entropy::{AntiEntropyConfig, AntiEntropyReconciler};\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::scatter::{FetchDocumentsRequest, NodeClient, WriteRequest};\nuse crate::task_store::TaskStore;\nuse crate::topology::Topology;\nuse reqwest::Client;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::hash::Hasher;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n/// Resharding operation state persisted in task store.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReshardState {\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    pub new_shards: u32,\n    pub phase: Phase,\n    pub shadow_index: Option<String>,\n    pub started_at: u64,\n    pub updated_at: u64,\n    pub backfill_progress: BackfillProgress,\n    pub verify_result: Option<VerifyResult>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct BackfillProgress {\n    pub total_documents: u64,\n    pub processed_documents: u64,\n    pub current_shard: Option<u32>,\n    pub last_cursor: Option<String>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct VerifyResult {\n    pub passed: bool,\n    pub mismatches: Vec<MismatchDetail>,\n    pub fingerprint_live: String,\n    pub fingerprint_shadow: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct MismatchDetail {\n    pub primary_key: String,\n    pub shard_old: u32,\n    pub shard_new: u32,\n    pub hash_live: Option<String>,\n    pub hash_shadow: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Resharding executor - It delegates to the shared [`crate::index_stats`] module so\n    /// that reshard and ILM rollover (plan §13.17) compute an index's document\n    /// count the *same* way: iterate the source `node_addresses`,\n    /// `GET /indexes/{uid}/stats`, and reduce `numberOfDocuments` with `max`\n    /// (each address hosts a full replica, so `sum` would over-count by the\n    /// replication factor).\n    ///\n    /// The aggregation is infallible from the caller's perspective — a node that\n    /// fails to respond (network / non-2xx / parse error) is logged and skipped,\n    /// and the count is `0` only if every node fails or reports zero — but this\n    /// returns [`Result`] to match the executor's method conventions and leave\n    /// the `start_backfill` caller (wired in a sibling bead) free to propagate a\n    /// future failure mode.\n    ///\n    /// Pure additive helper: this method is *not* called from the reshard state\n    /// machine in this bead.\n    pub async fn compute_source_document_count(&self, index_uid: &str) -> Result<u64> {\n        let stats = crate::index_stats::aggregate_index_stats(\n            &self.http_client,\n            &self.node_addresses,\n            &self.master_key,\n            index_uid,\n        )\n        .await;\n        Ok(stats.total_documents)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if backfill is complete.\n    async fn is_backfill_complete(&self, state: &ReshardState) -> Result<bool> {\n        Ok(state\n            .backfill_progress\n            .current_shard\n            .map(|s| s >= state.old_shards)\n            .unwrap_or(false))\n    }\n\n    /// Advance backfill by processing one shard.\n    ///\n    /// Implements plan §13.1 step 3: background streamer pages every live-index shard\n    /// using `filter=_miroir_shard={id}`. Each document is re-hashed under `S_new`\n    /// and written to the shadow. Documents are tagged with `_miroir_origin: reshard_backfill`\n    /// for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n    async fn advance_backfill(&self, state: &mut ReshardState) -> Result<()> {\n        let shard_id = state.backfill_progress.current_shard.unwrap_or(0);\n\n        debug!(\n            index = %state.index_uid,\n            shard = shard_id,\n            \"Starting backfill for shard\"\n        );\n\n        // Get a healthy node from topology\n        let topology = self.topology.read().await;\n        let node = topology.nodes().find(|n| n.is_healthy()).ok_or_else(|| {\n            MiroirError::Topology(\"No healthy nodes available for backfill\".to_string())\n        })?;\n        let node_id = node.id.clone();\n        let node_address = node.address.clone();\n        drop(topology);\n\n        // Get primary key field name\n        let primary_key = self.get_index_primary_key(&state.index_uid).await?;\n\n        // Get shadow index name\n        let shadow_name = state\n            .shadow_index\n            .as_ref()\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"Shadow index not created\".to_string()))?\n            .clone();\n\n        // Paginated fetch from live index with filter=_miroir_shard={shard_id}\n        let mut offset = 0u32;\n        let limit = self.config.backfill_batch_size as u32;\n        let mut total_processed = 0u64;\n        let mut total_docs_in_shard = 0u64;\n\n        loop {\n            let fetch_request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n                index_uid: state.index_uid.clone(),\n                filter: json!({ \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id }),\n                limit,\n                offset,\n            };\n\n            let fetch_response = self\n                .node_client\n                .fetch_documents(&node_id, &node_address, &fetch_request)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| match e {\n                    crate::scatter::NodeError::HttpError { status, body } => {\n                        MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                            \"fetch from node {node_id} failed: HTTP {status} - {body}\"\n                        ))\n                  ),\n                    crate::scatter::NodeError::Timeout => {\n                        MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"fetch from node {node_id} timed out\"))\n                    }\n                })?;\n\n            // Update total count on first page\n            if offset == 0 {\n                total_docs_in_shard = fetch_response.total;\n                state.backfill_progress.total_documents += total_docs_in_shard;\n            }\n\n            let docs = fetch_response.results;\n            let docs_in_page = docs.len() as u64;\n\n            if The up-front count this child introduces is the missing denominator that exists before the first page is fetched.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n- [ ] New helper (on the reshard executor and/or extracted from ilm.rs into a shared location) queries each healthy source node's `GET /indexes/{uid}/stats` (or an equivalent per-shard count via the `_miroir_shard={id}` filter) and returns an aggregated total document count.\n- [ ] It reuses/extracts the existing `ilm.rs` node-stats aggregation rather than duplicating the HTTP+aggregate logic.\n- [ ] Unit-tested against a mock node client returning known per-node counts.\n- [ ] No change to the reshard state machine or backfill migration logic (pure additive helper).\nAssignee: claude-code-glm-5-cgov\nLabels: deferred, split-child, umbrella","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4eedc800-3fad-4154-af09-dd04ccc87f9b","uuid":"ea40ad02-f1fa-47e4-8651-9d266f328ad1","timestamp":"2026-07-10T18:47:28.258Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"3b52aba refactor(miroir-core): migrate ILM onto shared index_stats module (bf-3r626)\nf10a675 feat(miroir-core): register index_stats module (bf-2r01e)\n---br status---\nID: bf-2ynu5\nTitle: Add per-node document-count query helper for reshard backfill\nStatus: in_progress\nPriority: P2\nType: task\nDescription: Split-child of bf-49a17 (reshard backfill progress never populated). This is the foundation piece: a reusable mechanism to compute a reshard source index's real document count, with no change to the reshard state machine.\n\n## Scope\n\nImplement a helper that computes the total document count for a reshard's source index across its source shards, so the backfill denominator is known before backfill begins. Reuse the existing per-node node-stats aggregation pattern rather than duplicating it.\n\n## Context (from bf-49a17 exploration)\n\n- `crates/miroir-core/src/ilm.rs:795` `fetch_index_stats` already does exactly this: it iterates node addresses, GETs `{address}/indexes/{uid}/stats`, and aggregates `node_stats.number_of_documents`. This is the precedent to reuse/extract.\n- `crates/miroir-core/src/reshard/executor.rs:513-530` `start_backfill` (Phase 3) currently hardcodes `total_documents: 0 // Will be updated`.\n- Note: `executor.rs:608-610` DOES accumulate `state.backfill_progress.total_documents += total_docs_in_shard` on the first page of each shard in `advance_backfill` — but that only populates the in-memory executor state incrementally, and never reaches the persisted `ReshardOperation.total_documents`. The up-front count this child introduces is the missing denominator that exists before the first page is fetched.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n\n- [ ] New helper (on the reshard executor and/or extracted from ilm.rs into a shared location) queries each healthy source node's `GET /indexes/{uid}/stats` (or an equivalent per-shard count via the `_miroir_shard={id}` filter) and returns an aggregated total document count.\n- [ ] It reuses/extracts the existing `ilm.rs` node-stats aggregation rather than duplicating the HTTP+aggregate logic.\n- [ ] Unit-tested against a mock node client returning known per-node counts.\n- [ ] No change to the reshard state machine or backfill migration logic (pure additive helper).\nAssignee: claude-code-glm-5-cgov\nLabels: deferred, split-child, umbrella","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

Shared per-node index-stats aggregation.\n//!\n//! Extracted from `ilm::fetch_index_stats` (plan §13.17) so that both ILM\n//! rollover evaluation and the reshard backfill denominator (bf-2ynu5) compute\n//! an index's real document count the *same* way: iterate the source node\n//! addresses, `GET /indexes/{uid}/stats`, and reduce `numberOfDocuments`.\n//!\n//! # Why `max`, not `sum`\n//!\n//! Each address in a coordinator's `node_addresses` hosts a full replica of the\n//! index (this is also why `reshard::advance_backfill` can read every shard of\n//! the source index from a single healthy node via `filter=_miroir_shard`).\n//! Reducing per-node counts with `max` therefore yields the true document count\n//! and tolerates a replica that is mid-ingest; `sum` would over-count by the\n//! replication factor. ILM relies on exactly this reduction for its `max_docs`\n//! rollover trigger, so the reshard denominator must agree with it.\n//!\n//! # Testability\n//!\n//! The HTTP transport ([`fetch_node_stats`]) is exercised end-to-end by the\n//! `miroir-proxy` ILM acceptance tests (mockito). The reduction policy itself is\n//! a pure function — [`reduce_document_counts`] — so it is unit-tested directly\n//! against known per-node counts (including failing nodes) without spinning up\n//! an HTTP server.\n\nuse reqwest::Client;\nuse serde::Deserialize;\nuse tracing::warn;\n\n/// `/indexes/{uid}/stats` response shape for a single node (Meilisearch).\n///\n/// Both fields default when absent so a minimal `{\"numberOfDocuments\": N}` body\n/// parses cleanly, and a node that lacks the index entirely yields zeros.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]\n#[allow(non_snake_case)]\npub struct NodeIndexStats {\n    #[serde(default)]\n    #[serde(rename = \"numberOfDocuments\")]\n    pub number_of_documents: u64,\n    /// Size detail — may be absent on older Meilisearch versions.\n    #[serde(rename = \"stats\", default)]\n    pub stats: Option<NodeStatsDetail>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Default)]\npub struct NodeStatsDetail {\n    #[serde(rename = \"databaseSize\", default)]\n    pub database_size: u64,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated index stats reduced across all source node addresses.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct IndexStats {\n    /// Document count, reduced with `max` across responding nodes.\n    pub total_documents: u64,\n    /// `databaseSize` summed across responding nodes.\n    pub total_size_bytes: u64,\n    /// Number of nodes that returned stats successfully (HTTP 2xx, parsable).\n    pub nodes_responded: usize,\n}\n\n/// Failure encountered while fetching stats from one node.\n#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]\npub enum FetchStatsError {\n    #[error(\"request failed: {0}\")]\n    Request(#[from] reqwest::Error),\n    #[error(\"failed to read response: {0}\")]\n    Read(reqwest::Error),\n    #[error(\"HTTP {status}: {body}\")]\n    Http { status: u16, body: String },\n    #[error(\"failed to parse stats: {0}\")]\n    Parse(serde_json::Error),\n}\n\n/// Fetch `/indexes/{uid}/stats` from a single node.\n///\n/// A 404 (index absent on that node) is reported as zero documents rather than\n/// an error, matching the original `ilm::fetch_node_stats` behavior — a node\n/// that has not yet received the index should not fail the aggregation.\npub async fn fetch_node_stats(\n    client: &Client,\n    address: &str,\n    master_key: &str,\n    index_uid: &str,\n) -> Result<NodeIndexStats, FetchStatsError> {\n    let url = format!(\n        \"{}/indexes/{}/stats\",\n        address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n        index_uid\n    );\n\n    let response = client\n        .get(&url)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", master_key))\n        .send()\n        .await\n        .map_err(FetchStatsError::Request)?;\n\n    let status = response.status();\n    let body_text = response\n        .text()\n        .await\n        .map_err(FetchStatsError::Read)?;\n\n    if status.as_u16() == 404 {\n        // Index doesn't exist on this node — count as zero, not an error.\n        return Ok(NodeIndexStats {\n            number_of_documents: 0,\n            stats: None,\n        });\n    }\n\n    if !status.is_success() {\n        return Err(FetchStatsError::Http {\n            status: status.as_u16(),\n            body: body_text,\n        });\n    }\n\n    serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(FetchStatsError::Parse)\n}\n\n/// Aggregate an index's stats across all source node addresses.\n///\n/// Iterates every address, GETs `/indexes/{uid}/stats`, and reduces the results\n/// the same way `ilm::fetch_index_stats` did: document count via `max`, size via\n/// `sum`. A node that errors (network / non-2xx / parse) is logged and skipped\n/// — one failing node never blocks the count. Returns zeros only if every node\n/// fails or reports zero.\npub async fn aggregate_index_stats(\n    client: &Client,\n    node_addresses: &[String],\n    master_key: &str,\n    index_uid: &str,\n) -> IndexStats {\n    let mut total_documents = 0u64;\n    let mut total_size_bytes = 0u64;\n    let mut nodes_responded = 0usize;\n\n    for address in node_addresses {\n        match fetch_node_stats(client, address, master_key, index_uid).await {\n            Ok(node_stats) => {\n                total_documents = total_documents.max(node_stats.number_of_documents);\n                if let Some(ref stats) = node_stats.stats {\n                    total_size_bytes += stats.database_size;\n                }\n                nodes_responded += 1;\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Log but continue - one node failing shouldn't block the count.\n                warn!(\"index_stats: failed to fetch stats from node {}: {}\", address, e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    IndexStats {\n        total_documents,\n        total_size_bytes,\n        nodes_responded,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Reduce per-node document counts into the index's real document count.\n///\n/// Pure (no I/O): takes each node's count as `Ok(n)` (or `Err(_)` if that node\n/// failed to respond) and returns the `max` across the responders, or `0` when\n/// no node responded. This is the policy shared by ILM rollover evaluation and\n/// the reshard backfill denominator, factored out so it can be unit-tested\n/// against known per-node counts without an HTTP server.\npub fn reduce_document_counts<I, E>(counts: I) -> u64\nwhere\n    I: IntoIterator<Item = Result<u64, E>>,\n{\n    counts\n        .into_iter()\n        .filter_map(Result::ok)\n        .max()\n        .unwrap_or(0)\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn parse_node_stats_full_body() {\n        let body = r#\"{\"numberOfDocuments\": 4242, \"stats\": {\"databaseSize\": 9999}}\"#;\n        let stats: NodeIndexStats = serde_json::from_str(body).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(stats.number_of_documents, 4242);\n        let detail = stats.stats.expect(\"stats detail present\");\n        assert_eq!(detail.database_size, 9999);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn parse_node_stats_missing_fields_default_zero() {\n        // A bare object must still parse, defaulting every field.\n        let stats: NodeIndexStats = serde_json::from_str(\"{}\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(stats.number_of_documents, 0);\n        assert!(stats.stats.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn parse_node_stats_docs_without_size_detail() {\n        let stats: NodeIndexStats = serde_json::from_str(r#\"{\"numberOfDocuments\": 7}\"#).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(stats.number_of_documents, 7);\n        assert!(stats.stats.is_none());\n    }\n\n    // --- reduce_document_counts: the \"mock node client\" aggregation seam ---\n    // Each `Ok(n)` is a node that returned `numberOfDocuments = n`; each `Err`\n    // is a node that failed to respond. The reduction must pick the maximum\n    // responder and tolerate any number of failures.\n\n    #[test]\n    fn reduce_picks_max_across_healthy_nodes() {\n        let counts: [Result<u64, ()>; 3] = [Ok(100), Ok(250), Ok(180)];\n        assert_eq!(reduce_document_counts(counts), 250);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn reduce_ignores_failed_nodes() {\n        // Two of four nodes failed; the denominator is still the max responder.\n        let counts: [Result<u64, ()>; 4] = [Ok(100), Err(()), Ok(250), Err(())];\n        assert_eq!(reduce_document_counts(counts), 250);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn reduce_zero_when_every_node_fails() {\n        let counts: [Result<u64, ()>; 3] = [Err(()), Err(()), Err(())];\n        assert_eq!(reduce_document_counts(counts), 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn reduce_zero_when_no_nodes() {\n        let counts: [Result<u64, ()>; 0] = [];\n        assert_eq!(reduce_document_counts(counts), 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn reduce_single_node() {\n        let counts: [Result<u64, ()>; 1] = [Ok(42)];\n        assert_eq!(reduce_document_counts(counts), 42);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn reduce_treats_zero_doc_index_as_zero() {\n        // An existing but empty index reports 0 on every node.\n        let counts: [Result<u64, ()>; 2] = [Ok(0), Ok(0)];\n        assert_eq!(reduce_document_counts(counts), 0);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":252,"startLine":1,"totalLines":252}}}

These tests cover the *other* seam: the\n    //! executor helper that drives the real reqwest transport against\n    //! `/indexes/{uid}/stats` via `index_stats::aggregate_index_stats`. Each\n    //! \"node\" is a mockito server returning a known `numberOfDocuments`, so we\n    //! assert the executor reduces them with `max`, tolerates a failing node,\n    //! and falls back to `0` when every node fails — matching how the\n    //! `miroir-proxy` ILM acceptance tests drive the same endpoint.\n\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::scatter::MockNodeClient;\n\n    /// Index uid every \"/indexes/test-idx/stats\";\n\n    /// Build an executor pointed at the given mockito node addresses.\n    ///\n    /// Only `node_addresses` (and the reqwest `http_client`) matter for\n    /// `compute_source_document_count`; the `node_client`/`topology`/`config`/\n    /// `task_store` are required by the constructor but untouched by the helper,\n    /// so they get inert defaults.\n    fn Arc::new(RwLock::new(Topology::new(2, 1, 1))),\n            ReshardConfig {\n                backfill_concurrency: 4,\n                backfill_batch_size: 100,\n                throttle_docs_per_sec: 0,\n                verify_before_swap: false,\n                retain_old_index_hours: 24,\n            },\n            Arc::new(MockNodeClient::default()),\n            None,\n            Arc::new(Client::new()),\n            Arc::new(node_addresses),\n            Arc::new(\"test-master-key\".to_string()),\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// Mount one `GET /indexes/{uid}/stats` mock returning `body` at `status`,\n    /// expected to be hit exactly once.\n    async fn mock_stats(\n        server: &mut mockito::Server,\n        status: usize,\n        body: serde_json::Value,\n    ) -> mockito::Mock {\n        server\n            .mock(\"GET\", STATS_PATH)\n            .with_status(status)\n            .with_body(body.to_string())\n            .expect(1)\n            .create_async()\n            .await\n    }\n\n    // (a) Multiple healthy nodes returning different counts → result is the max.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn compute_source_doc_count_returns_max_across_healthy_nodes() {\n        let mut server_a = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n        let mut server_b = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n        let mut server_c = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n        let mocks: Vec<mockito::Mock> = vec![\n            mock_stats(&mut server_a, 200, json!({\"numberOfDocuments\": 100})).await,\n            mock_stats(&mut server_b, 200, json!({\"numberOfDocuments\": 250})).await,\n            mock_stats(&mut server_c, 200, json!({\"numberOfDocuments\": 180})).await,\n        ];\n\n        let executor =\n            executor_with_nodes(vec![server_a.url(), server_b.url(), server_c.url()]);\n\n        let count = executor.compute_source_document_count(INDEX_UID).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(count, 250, \"denominator is the max responder, not the sum\");\n\n        // Prove we drove the real HTTP path: every node's stats endpoint was hit.\n        for m in &mocks {\n            m.assert_async().await;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // (b) One node failing (HTTP 5xx) → logged and skipped; max of the rest wins.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn compute_source_doc_count_ignores_failing_node() {\n        let mut server_a = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n        let mut server_dead = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n        let mut server_c = mockito::Server::new_async().await;\n\n        let mocks: Vec<mockito::Mock> = vec![\n            mock_stats(&mut server_a, 200, json!({\"numberOfDocuments\": 100})).await,\n            mock_stats(&mut server_dead, 500, json!({\"message\": \"internal error\"})).await,\n            mock_stats(&mut server_c, 200, json!({\"numberOfDocuments\": 250})).await,\n        ];\n\n        let executor = executor_with_nodes(vec![\n            server_a.url(),\n            server_dead.url(),\n            server_c.url(),\n        ]);\n\n        let count = executor.compute_source_document_count(INDEX_UID).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            count, 250,\n            \"a single failing node must not block the denominator\"\n        );\n\n
